title: U.S. orders Anthropic to suspend access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals
summary: The United States government forced the artificial intelligence company to restrict its most advanced models globally, generating an intense political and technological debate.

title: Complaints of toxic environment in Meta's AI unit and criticism of Zuckerberg
summary: Employees describe extremely negative working conditions in Meta's artificial intelligence team, while the company acknowledges strategic mistakes.

title: SpaceX goes public in record IPO
summary: Elon Musk's space company debuted on the stock market with a multi-billion dollar valuation, making its founder the first person with a trillion-dollar fortune.

title: Google sues Chinese network that used Gemini artificial intelligence to scam
summary: The lawsuit alleges that the criminal operation used Gemini AI to deceive hundreds of thousands of victims worldwide.

title: Malicious packages in Arch Linux compromise system security
summary: A malware campaign was detected that infected more than 1,500 packages in the Arch Linux AUR repository, including rootkits and information theft.

title: 2026 World Cup: news on matches, tickets, fans, and campaign
summary: Information and analysis on the most prominent aspects of the World Cup, from schedules and TV setup to campaign design and visitor experience.

title: Starlink renews its service with smaller antennas and adjusts prices in Spain
summary: SpaceX launches a new generation of more portable and autonomous Starlink antennas, while its competitiveness against traditional operators is analyzed.

title: Movistar Plus announces its summer movie premiere lineup
summary: The streaming platform presents the movies arriving in July and August, including Oscar-nominated titles.

title: Critical vulnerability in Splunk Enterprise allows unauthenticated remote code execution
summary: A security flaw in Splunk Enterprise can be exploited by attackers to execute malicious code without the need for credentials.

title: Chinese hackers hid backdoors in Linux systems for nearly a decade
summary: A Chinese cyberespionage group compromised Linux login software to maintain persistent access to government and corporate networks.

title: Microsoft considers spinning off its Xbox division amid rising hardware costs
summary: The company is evaluating the possibility of turning Xbox into a separate company to cope with rising console costs.

title: Jeff Bezos's AI startup raises $12 billion
summary: The artificial intelligence company founded by Jeff Bezos achieved one of the largest funding rounds in history.

title: French firm Mistral negotiates a €3 billion round at a €20 billion valuation
summary: Mistral, OpenAI's European competitor, is close to closing one of the largest investments in artificial intelligence.

title: OpenAI cuts prices for its Codex agent and triggers a price war in AI
summary: The company adjusts the pricing limits of its Codex model to compete in the artificial intelligence agent market.

title: CRISPR technique selectively destroys cancer cells, including those considered incurable
summary: Researchers have used gene editing to target and eliminate tumor cells, opening new possibilities in oncology.

title: 21 zero-day vulnerabilities discovered in FFmpeg video codec
summary: Researchers reported multiple critical flaws in the FFmpeg multimedia library that allow remote code execution.

title: FBI builds miniature city to train in cyber defense
summary: The U.S. federal agency created a replica of a small town to simulate and study real-world cyber attacks.

title: Court finds Google liable for false claims generated by AI Overviews
summary: A U.S. judge ruled that Google can be sued for damages caused by incorrect responses from its artificial intelligence in search results.

title: Survey reveals more than half of Americans fear losing their jobs to AI
summary: A study shows that 51% of U.S. citizens are worried about the impact of artificial intelligence on their career and their ability to think critically.

title: Sam Bankman-Fried loses appeal, upholds conviction for FTX collapse
summary: The founder of the FTX cryptocurrency platform failed to overturn his conviction for massive fraud, which includes long prison sentences.
